meta.source: Indian Consensus Guidelines on Adult Immunization 2026

columns.at_risk: At-Risk (DM, Heart, Liver, CRD, CKD, Nephrotic syndrome, Haemodialysis)
columns.high_risk_immuno: High-Risk / Immunocompromised (HIV, Malignancies, Transplant, Asplenia, SCD, Down syndrome, CSF leak, Cochlear implant, Complement deficiencies)
columns.special_situations: Special Situations (HCPs, Travellers, Mass gatherings, Occupational exposure)

recommendation_legend.BR: May be considered after benefit-risk evaluation
recommendation_legend.AR: With additional risk / shared clinical decision-making

vaccines.name: Influenza Vaccine
vaccines.schedule: Annual, 1 dose every year
contraindications: Severe allergic reaction to previous influenza vaccine
Egg allergy — use egg-free or cell-based formulation
precautions: GBS (Guillain-Barré Syndrome) within 6 weeks of prior influenza vaccination
Administer throughout influenza season (pre-monsoon in South India; pre-winter in North India)

special_populations.pregnancy: Recommended in any trimester — safe and protective for mother and infant
special_populations.immunocompromised: Inactivated vaccine only — tetravalent preferred; close contacts should also receive tetravalent

vaccines.booster: Annual repeat dose
vaccines.co_administration: Can be co-administered with most other vaccines

vaccines.name: COVID-19 Vaccine
vaccines.schedule: Primary series: 2 doses (Day 0 + 28 days, or as per specific vaccine schedule); Booster: single dose at 12 months
contraindications: Severe allergic reaction to previous COVID-19 vaccine or its components
precautions: Recommended during pandemic/epidemic situations
Booster for elderly and significant comorbidities: at least 12 months after previous dose
No head-to-head comparison between vaccines — use locally available approved vaccine

special_populations.pregnancy: Strongly recommended during pandemic — 2 doses at least 4 weeks apart
special_populations.immunocompromised: Recommended; additional doses may be needed
special_populations.elderly: Additional booster doses for all older adults

vaccines.booster: Single booster dose 12 months after primary series; additional doses for high-priority groups

vaccines.name: Pneumococcal Vaccine
vaccines.schedule: Preferred: Single dose PCV20 OR PCV13 followed by PPSV23 (1 year later for ≥50 yrs; 8 weeks later for high-risk)
precautions: Previously vaccinated: single dose PCV20 1 year later
PCV13+PPSV23 previously given: 1 dose PCV20 at 5 years after last PPSV23 dose
PPSV23 booster: 1 dose every 5 years for high-risk

special_populations.immunocompromised: PCV13 + PPSV23 8 weeks apart (high-risk accelerated schedule)
special_populations.elderly: All adults ≥50 years: single dose PCV20 (preferred)

vaccines.booster: PPSV23: 1 dose every 5 years for high-risk

vaccines.name: Hepatitis B Vaccine
vaccines.schedule: 3 doses: 0, 1, 6 months (standard); Accelerated: 0, 7, 21–30 days + booster at 12 months (combined HepA+HepB)
vaccines.doses: 3 doses (standard); CKD/dialysis: 4 doses (0, 2, 6, 12 months) at 40 mcg
vaccines.route: Deep IM in deltoid region (avoid buttocks)
precautions: Booster: calculate based on antibody titres; revaccination every 5 years for high-risk (HCW, CKD on haemodialysis, immunocompromised)
CKD/dialysis: higher dose (40 mcg), 4-dose schedule (0, 2, 6, 12 months)

special_populations.pregnancy: Recommended for unvaccinated at-risk pregnant women
special_populations.immunocompromised: Check post-vaccination titres; revaccinate if non-responder
special_populations.ckd_dialysis: 40 mcg (1 ml) at 0, 2, 6, and 12 months; booster every 5 years

vaccines.booster: Revaccination every 5 years for high-risk (HCW, immunocompromised, CKD on haemodialysis)

vaccines.name: Hepatitis A Vaccine
vaccines.schedule: Inactivated: 2 doses, 6 months apart (1 ml IM); Live: single dose (0.5 ml SC)
contraindications: Pregnancy: contraindicated (use only inactivated if absolutely necessary with risk discussion)
precautions: Post-exposure vaccination recommended for MSM and other high-risk situations
Combined HepA+HepB vaccine: accelerated schedule 0, 7, 21–30 days + booster at 12 months
≥50 years: only inactivated vaccine recommended

special_populations.pregnancy: With additional risk (AR) — contraindicated per key considerations; inactivated only if needed

vaccines.booster: Not required

vaccines.name: HPV Vaccine (Human Papillomavirus)
vaccines.schedule: Age 9–14: 2 doses at least 5 months apart; Age ≥15: 3 doses at 0, 1–2, 5–8 months; Immunocompromised (any age): 3 doses
vaccines.doses: 2 doses (if started age 9–14) OR 3 doses (if started ≥15 years)
formulations: HPV2 (Bivalent — Cervarix): protects against HPV 16 and 18
precautions: All people up to age 26: recommended
Adults 27–45: shared clinical decision-making (BR category)
Immunocompromised (including HIV): 3-dose series regardless of starting age
Best administered before first sexual encounter

special_populations.pregnancy: Not recommended — defer until after delivery (no evidence of teratogenicity if inadvertently given)
special_populations.immunocompromised: 3-dose series even if started at 9–14 years

vaccines.booster: Not required

vaccines.name: Tdap / Td Vaccine (Tetanus, Diphtheria, Pertussis)
vaccines.schedule: Unvaccinated adults: 3 doses (Tdap at 0, Td/Tdap at 4 weeks, Td/Tdap at 6–12 months); Booster: single Td or Tdap every 10 years
precautions: Each pregnancy: 1 dose Tdap at 27–36 weeks gestation
Wound management: if not fully immunized, start primary series
After injury with tetanus risk: Tdap booster even if vaccinated, if >5 years since last dose

special_populations.pregnancy: Tdap in EVERY pregnancy at 27–36 weeks gestation (provides neonatal protection)
special_populations.wound: 1 dose Tdap if wound with tetanus risk and >5 years since last dose

vaccines.booster: 1 dose Td or Tdap every 10 years; additionally in pregnancy (weeks 27–36)

vaccines.name: Herpes Zoster Vaccine (Shingles)
vaccines.schedule: 2 doses, 2–6 months apart
formulations: Recombinant Zoster Vaccine (RZV/Shingrix) — non-live, can be used in immunocompromised
contraindications: Do not expose to direct sunlight or heat
precautions: Recommended for all adults ≥50 years, also to prevent recurrence of herpes zoster
Recommended in patients with CVD (MI, CHF, Stroke) — bidirectional risk with HZ

special_populations.immunocompromised: Recombinant vaccine (non-live) — can be administered to immunocompromised patients
special_populations.cvd: Recommended — bidirectional risk between herpes zoster and cardiovascular disease

vaccines.booster: Not required

vaccines.name: MMR Vaccine (Measles, Mumps, Rubella)
vaccines.schedule: 2 doses, 4 weeks apart
precautions: Rubella-susceptible women not pregnant: give 1 dose MMR; if pregnant, administer postpartum
If inadvertently given during pregnancy: do NOT terminate pregnancy (no proven teratogenic risk)
MMR safe during breastfeeding
HCW without evidence of immunity: prioritize vaccination

special_populations.pregnancy: Contraindicated; administer postpartum. OK during measles/rubella outbreaks if benefit > risk
special_populations.immunocompromised: Avoid in severely immunocompromised; consult specialist

vaccines.booster: Not required

vaccines.name: Varicella Vaccine (Chickenpox)
vaccines.schedule: 2 doses, 4–8 weeks apart; post-exposure: within 3–5 days of exposure
precautions: Post-exposure: within 3–5 days of exposure to prevent or modify disease
Pre-vaccinated (1 dose): 1 additional dose; not vaccinated: 2 doses
High-risk groups: HCW, household contacts of immunocompromised persons

special_populations.pregnancy: Not recommended; vaccinate susceptible women postpartum
special_populations.healthcare_workers: Recommended for susceptible HCW (occupational exposure risk)

vaccines.booster: Not required (post-exposure: see schedule)

vaccines.name: Meningococcal Vaccine
vaccines.schedule: Single dose IM; high-risk adults: different dosing schedule
vaccines.doses: 1 dose standard; multiple doses for high-risk
formulations: MenACWY (Quadrivalent): protects against serogroups A, C, W, Y
MenB (Serogroup B): protects against serogroup B
precautions: High-risk adults (asplenia, HIV, complement deficiency): different dosing schedule
Mass gatherings (Hajj, Kumbh Mela): MenACWY recommended

special_populations.asplenia: MenACWY: R; MenB: R — both recommended
special_populations.travel: MenACWY: R for travel to endemic/hyperendemic areas; Hajj: MenACWY mandatory

vaccines.booster: MenACWY: every 5 years if risk continues; MenB: 1 booster 1 year after primary, then every 2–3 years if risk continues

vaccines.name: Typhoid Vaccine
vaccines.schedule: Inactivated ViPS/TCV: single dose IM at least 2 weeks before travel; Live oral: 4 capsules on Days 1, 3, 5, 7 at least 1 week before travel
formulations: Typhoid Conjugate Vaccine (TCV): injectable IM (preferred, longer protection)
Live attenuated oral vaccine (Ty21a): 4 capsules, Days 1, 3, 5, 7
contraindications: Live oral vaccine contraindicated in pregnancy
precautions: Inactivated vaccine preferred in pregnancy (live oral vaccine contraindicated)
ViPS booster: every 2 years if risk continues
Live oral booster: every 5 years if risk continues

special_populations.pregnancy: Only inactivated (ViPS/TCV) vaccine; live oral vaccine contraindicated
special_populations.travel: Administer at least 2 weeks (ViPS/TCV) or 1 week (live oral) before travel

vaccines.booster: ViPS: every 2 years; Live oral: every 5 years (if risk continues)

vaccines.name: Hepatitis E Vaccine
vaccines.schedule: 3 doses: 0, 1, 6 months
precautions: Pregnancy: based on shared clinical decision-making
Special situations include women of childbearing age, travellers to endemic areas, animal husbandry/catering workers, military, outbreaks

special_populations.pregnancy: Shared clinical decision-making — benefit vs risk evaluation

vaccines.booster: Not required

vaccines.name: Japanese Encephalitis (JE) Vaccine
vaccines.schedule: Live attenuated: 2 doses 7–28 days apart (0.5 ml SC); Inactivated Vero cell: 2 doses Day 0 and Day 28 (0.5 ml SC)
formulations: Inactivated Vero cell-derived vaccine (approved for ≥2 months — preferred for travelers)
contraindications: Live vaccine: do not administer during epidemic season
precautions: Travellers staying ≥1 month in endemic areas during transmission season
Short-term travellers (<1 month): consider if outdoor/rural activities, uncertain itinerary
Not recommended for urban-only short-term travellers
Kumbh Mela: consider if stay >1 month
Chronic illness/immunodeficiency in endemic areas: R

special_populations.pregnancy: Weigh benefit-risk carefully; inactivated vaccine preferred

vaccines.booster: Live attenuated: after 11 months for high-risk; Inactivated: after 1–2 years if ongoing exposure

vaccines.name: Rabies Vaccine (Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis)
vaccines.schedule: Pre-exposure (PrEP): 3 doses at Day 0, 7, 21–28; Post-exposure (PEP): 5 doses at Day 0, 3, 7, 14, 28
vaccines.doses: PrEP: 3 doses; PEP: 5 doses (IM) or 4 doses (ID)
vaccines.route: IM (intramuscular) standard; Intradermal (ID) for resource-limited settings
vaccines.dose_volume: PVRV: 0.5 ml IM; HDCV/PCEC/PDEC: 1 ml IM; ID: 0.1 ml
formulations: PCEC: Purified Chick Embryo Cell Vaccine
PDEC: Purified Duck Embryo Cell Vaccine
PVRV: Purified Vero Cell Rabies Vaccine (most common in India)
precautions: Always follow National Guidelines for Rabies Prophylaxis (NCDC/WHO)
Immediate wound care essential regardless of vaccination status
RIG (Rabies Immunoglobulin) required for severe exposures
Re-exposure after completed PrEP and PEP: 2 booster doses at Day 0 and Day 3

special_populations.pregnancy: PEP always indicated; PrEP: R if exposure risk
special_populations.veterinarians: PrEP mandatory for veterinarians, animal handlers, cave explorers, rabies lab workers

vaccines.booster: 2 doses at Day 0 and Day 3 if re-exposed after completed prophylaxis

vaccines.name: Polio Vaccine (IPV/OPV)
vaccines.schedule: Unvaccinated/incompletely vaccinated: 3 doses (Day 0, +1 month, +6–12 months); Previously vaccinated: 1 lifetime booster IPV
formulations: IPV (Inactivated Polio Vaccine): injectable, ID (note: not available as single injection in India)
OPV (Oral Polio Vaccine): live attenuated, highly heat-sensitive
contraindications: OPV: not available as routine for adults in India; IPV preferred
precautions: IPV not available as single injection in India
Travellers to countries with ongoing polio epidemic or endemic
HCW and lab workers handling poliovirus specimens
OPV: store frozen, highly heat-sensitive

special_populations.pregnancy: IPV safe; consider if travel to endemic area

vaccines.booster: 1 lifetime booster IPV for previously vaccinated adults

vaccines.name: Hib Vaccine (Haemophilus influenzae type b)
vaccines.schedule: At-risk adults: 1 or 3 doses depending on indication
vaccines.doses: 1 dose (most at-risk adults); 3 doses at 4-week intervals (HSCT recipients)
precautions: Elective splenectomy: 1 dose at least 14 days before surgery (if unvaccinated)
Functional/anatomic asplenia: 1 dose if unvaccinated
HSCT recipients: 3 doses at 4-week intervals, 6–12 months post-transplant

special_populations.hsct: 3 doses at 4-week intervals, regardless of prior Hib history

vaccines.booster: Not required

vaccines.name: Yellow Fever Vaccine
vaccines.schedule: Single dose, at least 10 days before travel to endemic areas
vaccines.route: IM in deltoid or anterolateral thigh
contraindications: Pregnancy: usually avoid; if travel unavoidable, benefit-risk evaluation
precautions: Required for entry into many African and South American nations
Must be administered at designated Yellow Fever Vaccination Centres (Govt of India certified)
Administer at least 10 days before travel
Booster recommended if: vaccinated before 9 months of age, immunosuppressive therapy, HIV/AIDS, lab workers handling wild-type virus

special_populations.pregnancy: Usually not recommended; postpone travel if possible; 1 dose after thorough benefit-risk evaluation if unavoidable
special_populations.lab_workers: Booster recommended for lab workers handling wild-type yellow fever virus

vaccines.booster: Single dose provides lifelong protection in most; specific groups require booster (see precautions)

vaccines.name: Cholera Vaccine
vaccines.schedule: WC-rBS: 2 doses ≥1 week before exposure; BivWC: 2 doses 2 weeks apart; Live CVD 103-HgR: single dose ≤10 days before exposure
formulations: Killed WC-rBS (O1 + cholera toxin B subunit): 2 doses ≥1 week apart
BivWC (O1 and O139, without cholera toxin B subunit): 2 doses 2 weeks apart
Live attenuated CVD 103-HgR: single dose ≤10 days before exposure
precautions: Not for routine use
Travellers at high risk, limited healthcare access areas
Natural calamity/outbreak: 2 doses at least 2 weeks apart

special_populations.pregnancy: Live vaccine contraindicated; inactivated vaccine may be considered

vaccines.booster: Endemic/outbreak situations: booster dose after 2 years

vaccines.name: BCG Vaccine (Tuberculosis)
vaccines.schedule: Single intradermal dose
contraindications: Generally not administered above age 16 years (limited evidence of efficacy in adults)
precautions: For occupational groups aged 16–35: HCW, veterinary staff, abattoir workers at TB risk
Re-vaccination in adults may offer added protection for vulnerable populations
Do not expose to direct sunlight or heat

special_populations.healthcare_workers: BCG may be considered for tuberculin-negative HCW aged 16–35
special_populations.immunocompromised: Live vaccine — avoid in severely immunocompromised

vaccines.booster: Re-vaccination may be considered for vulnerable populations

vaccines.name: Anthrax Vaccine
vaccines.schedule: Pre-exposure: 3 doses at 0, 1, 6 months IM; Post-exposure: 3 doses at 0, 2, 4 weeks SC
precautions: Pre-exposure booster: at 6 and 12 months after primary, then 12-monthly if risk persists
Only for individuals at increased risk of exposure (livestock handlers in endemic areas, lab workers)

special_populations.lab_workers: Pre-exposure recommended for those at risk of exposure to Bacillus anthracis

vaccines.booster: Pre-exposure: boosters at 6 and 12 months, then annually if risk persists

ckd.label: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) / Dialysis
ckd.notes: HepB: 40 mcg (1 ml) dose at 0, 2, 6, 12 months for dialysis patients

hiv.label: HIV / AIDS
hiv.notes: Avoid live vaccines (MMR, Varicella, Yellow Fever) unless CD4 adequate

immunocompromised.label: Immunocompromised (Malignancy, Transplant, Chemotherapy)
immunocompromised.notes: Avoid all live vaccines. Timing matters: vaccinate before immunosuppression if possible

asplenia.label: Asplenia (Functional or Anatomical)
asplenia.notes: All encapsulated bacteria vaccines essential: Pneumococcal + MenACWY + Hib

pregnancy.label: Pregnancy
pregnancy.notes: Tdap at every pregnancy (27–36 weeks). Live vaccines contraindicated.

travel.label: Travellers
travel.notes: Vaccines depend on destination — check country-specific requirements

page_52_chart.title: Age-wise Vaccination Recommendation According to Risk
page_52_chart.description: Master color-coded recommendation chart for all 23 vaccines across 6 patient risk categories

co_administration_rules.general: Most inactivated vaccines can be given simultaneously
co_administration_rules.live_vaccines: Live vaccines (MMR, Varicella, Yellow Fever, JE live) should ideally be given same day or ≥4 weeks apart
exceptions: Live attenuated vaccines: same day or ≥4 weeks interval between them

missed_dose_rules.general: Do not restart series — continue from where left off
missed_dose_rules.influenza: Annual dose — if missed, vaccinate whenever available during flu season
missed_dose_rules.hep_b: Continue series; do not restart even if interval prolonged
missed_dose_rules.tdap: Continue from where interrupted; use minimum intervals
